Version
  • Library
  • Library

Library Reference

Complete reference for all exported types, functions, and constants. Use the search bar (top right) to find any symbol.

Weather Data

Types

Main.GreenhousesJL.TMYData — Type
TMYData

Hourly Typical Meteorological Year data, loaded from a Greenhouses-format text file.

Fields

  • time::Vector{Float64} — time [s] since simulation start
  • T_out::Vector{Float64} — outdoor dry-bulb temperature [°C]
  • RH_out::Vector{Float64} — outdoor relative humidity [%]
  • P_out::Vector{Float64} — atmospheric pressure [Pa]
  • I_glob::Vector{Float64} — global horizontal irradiance [W/m²]
  • u_wind::Vector{Float64} — wind speed at 10 m [m/s]
  • T_sky::Vector{Float64} — sky temperature [°C]

Functions

Main.GreenhousesJL.load_tmy — Function
load_tmy(filepath::String) → TMYData

Load a Greenhouses-format TMY text file. Lines starting with # or double are skipped. Columns: time, T_out, RH_out, P_out, I_glob, u_wind, T_sky, [T_air_sp, CO2_sp, ilu_sp].

Main.GreenhousesJL.interp_tmy — Function
interp_tmy(tmy::TMYData, t::Float64) → (T_out, I_glob, u_wind, T_sky)

Linearly interpolate weather data at time t [s].

Returns

  • T_out — outdoor temperature [°C]
  • I_glob — global irradiance [W/m²]
  • u_wind — wind speed [m/s]
  • T_sky — sky temperature [°C]

Greenhouse Parameters

Types

Main.GreenhousesJL.GreenhouseParams — Type
GreenhouseParams

Complete parameter set for a greenhouse thermal simulation.

Holds geometry, material properties, soil model, wall model, heating controller, PV panel, crop model, and weather data. Use the convenience constructors GreenhouseParams(tmy; ...) or SavonaParams(tmy; ...) instead of building this struct directly.

Key fields

  • A — floor area [m²]
  • ϕ — roof slope [rad]
  • h_air — air zone mean height [m]
  • panel — PV panel (PVPanel)
  • crop — crop model (CropModel or nothing)
  • Kp, Ti, T_sp, Q_max — PI heating controller parameters
Main.GreenhousesJL.SoilParams — Type
SoilParams(N_c, N_s, λ_c, λ_s, ρcp_c, ρcp_s, A, T_deep)

Multi-layer soil conduction model parameters.

Fields

  • N_c::Int — number of concrete layers (near surface)
  • N_s::Int — number of soil layers (deeper)
  • λ_c::Float64 — concrete thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)]
  • λ_s::Float64 — soil thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)]
  • ρcp_c::Float64 — concrete volumetric heat capacity [J/(m³·K)]
  • ρcp_s::Float64 — soil volumetric heat capacity [J/(m³·K)]
  • A::Float64 — floor area [m²]
  • T_deep::Float64 — undisturbed deep ground temperature [K]

T_deep represents the ground temperature at sufficient depth (≥ 10 m) where annual seasonal swing is negligible (< 2 %). At that depth, T ≈ annual mean surface temperature (Kusuda & Achenbach 1965; Fossa, UniGe DIME 2023). The soil discretisation (layers doubling from 0.02 m: total ~1.3 m) handles the near-surface thermal dynamics; T_deep is the constant boundary at the bottom.

Constructors

Main.GreenhousesJL.GreenhouseParams — Method

Create params for the 14000m² bare/heated greenhouse.

When T_deep is left as nothing (the default), the deep ground boundary temperature is set to the annual mean of the supplied TMY (mean(tmy.T_out) + 273.15). This represents the undisturbed ground temperature at depth ≥ 10 m where annual seasonal swing is < 2 % (Kusuda & Achenbach 1965; Fossa, UniGe DIME 2023).

A hard-coded value of 3 °C (the original Modelica default for Brussels winter) silently turns the soil into a heat sink and underestimates summer overheating by up to +13 °C (see Impact 1 in the presentation).

Main.GreenhousesJL.SavonaParams — Function

Create params for Savona 5x5m with walls.

T_deep defaults to the annual mean of the supplied TMY (same convention as GreenhouseParams). At depths ≥ 10 m the ground temperature equals the annual mean surface temperature with < 2 % seasonal swing (Kusuda & Achenbach 1965; Fossa, Solar and Geothermal Energy, UniGe DIME 2023). The soil discretisation extends ~1.3 m; the boundary condition at the bottom of the last layer represents the connection to this undisturbed deep ground.

Query functions

Main.GreenhousesJL.state_size — Function
state_size(p) → Int

Length of the ODE state vector for this greenhouse configuration. Depends on: walls (+1), crop (+1), growth (+3), pipes (+1), PCM (+1), heating (+1), PV (+1).

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_walls — Function
has_walls(p) → Bool

Whether the greenhouse includes wall heat losses (A_walls > 0).

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_heating — Function
has_heating(p) → Bool

Whether PI heating is active (Kp > 0).

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_pv — Function
has_pv(p) → Bool

Whether PV panels are mounted on the greenhouse.

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_crop — Function
has_crop(p) → Bool

Whether a crop model is attached to the greenhouse.

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_growth — Function
has_growth(p) → Bool

Whether the crop has dynamic LAI growth enabled.

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_cycle — Function
has_cycle(crop) → Bool

Whether the crop has harvest/replanting cycles (cycle_days > 0 and dynamic_LAI = true).

Main.GreenhousesJL.n_soil — Function
n_soil(p) → Int

Number of soil temperature states (concrete + soil layers).

PV Panels

Types

Main.GreenhousesJL.PVPanel — Type
PVPanel(τ, α, η, coverage, facade, tilt_extra)

Photovoltaic panel mounted ON TOP of the greenhouse glass.

Fields

  • τ::Float64 — solar transmittance (0 = opaque, 1 = clear glass) [-]
  • α::Float64 — solar absorptance (fraction of radiation absorbed → heat + electricity) [-]
  • η::Float64 — electrical efficiency at STC 25°C [-]
  • coverage::Float64 — fraction of facade area covered by panels (0–1) [-]
  • facade::Symbol — which surface: :roof, :south_wall, :north_wall, :all_walls
  • tilt_extra::Float64 — extra tilt angle above the mounting surface [rad] (0 = flush)

Tilt angle

Total panel tilt = facade tilt + tilt_extra. For roof-mounted panels, the minimum tilt equals the roof slope (panels cannot tilt below the mounting surface). Tilt affects: solar incidence angle (cos θ correction on I_glob), exterior convection (wind exposure changes with tilt), and the view factor to the sky (radiosity).

Energy balance on covered area

  • Transmitted to interior: τ × I_glob × cos(θ_panel)
  • Electricity: α × η(T_cell) × I_glob × cos(θ_panel)
  • Waste heat to glass below: α × (1 − η) × I_glob × cos(θ_panel)
  • Reflected: (1 − τ − α) × I_glob

Presets

Main.GreenhousesJL.NoPanel — Function

No panel (default — bare glass roof).

Main.GreenhousesJL.OpaquePanel — Function
OpaquePanel(; coverage=1.0, η=0.20, facade=:roof)

Standard crystalline silicon panel. Blocks all light (τ=0).

  • coverage: fraction of the facade covered (0–1). Default: 100% of roof.
  • η: electrical efficiency at STC (default 20%).
Main.GreenhousesJL.SemiTransparentPanel — Function
SemiTransparentPanel(; coverage=1.0, τ=0.40, η=0.10, facade=:roof)

Thin-film or organic PV. Each panel lets τ fraction of light through.

  • τ: panel transmittance (default 40% — each panel lets 40% of light through).
  • coverage: fraction of roof covered (default 100%).
  • η: electrical efficiency at STC.
Main.GreenhousesJL.TransparentPanel — Function
TransparentPanel(; coverage=1.0, τ=0.65, η=0.06, facade=:roof)

BIPV transparent glass. High light transmission, low efficiency.

  • τ: panel transmittance (default 65%).
  • coverage: fraction of roof covered (default 100%).
  • η: electrical efficiency at STC.

Physics

Main.GreenhousesJL.pv_cell_temperature — Function
pv_cell_temperature(T_out, I_glob, u_wind, panel) → T_cell [°C]

Cell temperature using the Faiman (2008) model:

T_cell = T_out + I_glob / (U₀ + U₁ × u_wind)

where U₀ = 25 W/(m²·K) and U₁ = 6.84 W·s/(m³·K) are empirical coefficients for a roof-mounted glass-glass module.

Reference: Faiman, D. (2008). Assessing the outdoor operating temperature of photovoltaic modules. Progress in Photovoltaics, 16(4), 307–315.

Main.GreenhousesJL.pv_efficiency — Function
pv_efficiency(η_stc, T_cell) → η

Temperature-corrected PV efficiency.

η(T) = η_STC × [1 + β × (T_cell − 25)]

β is the temperature coefficient:

  • Crystalline Si: β = −0.0040 /°C (−0.40%/°C)
  • Thin-film CdTe: β = −0.0020 /°C
  • Organic PV: β = −0.0025 /°C

The coefficient is selected automatically based on the panel transmittance: τ = 0 → crystalline Si, τ > 0 → thin-film/organic.

Main.GreenhousesJL.solar_absorbed — Function
solar_absorbed(I_glob, panel, T_out, u_wind; LAI=0)
    → (R_SunCov, R_SunFlr, R_SunCan, Q_pv_heat, P_elec, T_cell, R_SunAir)

Compute solar radiation distribution through the greenhouse envelope and canopy. This is a Julia port of Greenhouses.Components.Greenhouse.Solar_model.mo.

Returns (all in W/m² of floor area)

  • R_SunCov: shortwave absorbed by the glass cover
  • R_SunFlr: shortwave reaching and absorbed by the floor
  • R_SunCan: shortwave absorbed by the canopy (PAR + NIR, multi-layer model)
  • Q_pv_heat: waste heat from PV panel (warms the cover)
  • P_elec: electrical power generated by PV panel
  • T_cell: PV cell temperature [°C]
  • R_SunAir: shortwave released to the air (ηglobair × ...)

Crop Models

Types

Main.GreenhousesJL.CropModel — Type
CropModel(; LAI_0, r_s0, SLA, ...)

Canopy parameters for coupling with the greenhouse energy balance. Supports dynamic LAI growth (Vanthoor source-sink model) when dynamic_LAI=true.

Growth model (simplified Vanthoor 2011):

  • Photosynthesis → carbohydrate buffer (C_Buf)
  • Buffer → leaves (CLeaf), stems (CStem)
  • LAI = SLA × C_Leaf
  • Leaf pruning when LAI > LAI_max

Presets

Main.GreenhousesJL.LettuceCrop — Function

Lettuce: vegetative, cold-tolerant, 30-day cycle. Tbase=4°C, Tceil=30°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.CucumberCrop — Function

Cucumber: warm-season, frost-sensitive, 120-day cycle. Tbase=12°C, Tceil=38°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.PepperCrop — Function

Pepper: warm-season, similar to tomato. Tbase=10°C, Tceil=35°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.StrawberryCrop — Function

Strawberry: moderate temps. Tbase=5°C, Tceil=32°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.BasilCrop — Function

Basil: warm-season herb, fast growth. Tbase=10°C, Tceil=38°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.SpinachCrop — Function

Spinach: cold-tolerant, bolts above 22°C. Tbase=2°C, Tceil=25°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.RoseCrop — Function

Rose: ornamental, moderate temps. Tbase=5°C, Tceil=33°C.

Main.GreenhousesJL.CannabisCrop — Function

Cannabis: controlled environment. Tbase=12°C, Tceil=35°C.

Growth & Energy Balance

Main.GreenhousesJL.get_LAI — Function

Get current LAI from crop model (static or from state), accounting for fallow.

Main.GreenhousesJL.growth_rates — Function
growth_rates(T_can, C_Buf, C_Leaf, An, crop) → (dC_Buf, dC_Leaf, dC_Stem)

Simplified Vanthoor (2011) source-sink growth model.

  • Photosynthesis → buffer
  • Buffer → leaves + stems (temperature-dependent)
  • Respiration (growth + maintenance)

Returns derivatives in mg_CH2O/(m²·s).

Main.GreenhousesJL.cycle_phase — Function
cycle_phase(t, crop) → (:growing, days_in_phase) or (:fallow, days_in_phase)

Determine if the crop is in growing or fallow phase at time t [s].

Main.GreenhousesJL.canopy_fluxes — Function
canopy_fluxes(T_air, T_canopy, T_cover, RH_air, PAR, CO2, u_air, crop;
              use_plantbiophysics=false) → (H, λE, Aₙ)

Compute canopy heat and mass fluxes.

Returns (W/m²_floor):

  • H: sensible heat from canopy to air
  • λE: latent heat (transpiration)
  • Aₙ: net CO2 assimilation [µmol/m²_floor/s]

Set use_plantbiophysics=true to use PlantBiophysics.jl (must be loaded).

Main.GreenhousesJL.canopy_energy_balance_stanghellini — Function
canopy_energy_balance_stanghellini(
    T_air, T_canopy, T_cover, RH_air, PAR_canopy,
    CO2_ppm, u_air, crop) → (H, λE, Aₙ)

Simplified Stanghellini (1987) canopy energy balance.

Returns (all in W/m²_floor):

  • H: sensible heat flux canopy → air (positive = canopy heats air)
  • λE: latent heat flux (transpiration, positive = canopy loses water)
  • Aₙ: net CO2 assimilation [µmol CO2/m²/s]
Main.GreenhousesJL.sat_vp — Function

Saturated vapour pressure [Pa] from temperature [°C] (Magnus formula).

Insulation

Night Covers

Main.GreenhousesJL.NightCover — Type
NightCover(; R_value, deploy_below_irradiance, name)

Movable insulating blanket deployed over the glass roof at night. Common in Chinese solar greenhouses.

When deployed, adds thermal resistance R_value [m²·K/W] to the roof, reducing convective and radiative losses. Deployed automatically when I_glob < deploy_below_irradiance.

Presets

  • NoCover() — no cover (default)
  • ThinBlanket() — polyester blanket, R=0.5
  • ThickBlanket() — quilted cotton, R=1.5
  • InsulatedMat() — foam + reflective, R=3.0
  • ChineseCover() — traditional straw mat + plastic, R=2.0

Physics

With cover deployed, the effective U-value of the roof becomes:

1/U_eff = 1/U_glass + R_cover
Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for NoCover. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for ThinBlanket. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for ThickBlanket. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for InsulatedMat. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for ChineseCover. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for has_cover. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for is_deployed. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Main.GreenhousesJL.cover_factor — Function
cover_factor(cover, I_glob) → (f_conv, f_rad)

Reduction factors for convective and radiative losses when cover is deployed. Returns (1.0, 1.0) when cover is open (daytime).

  • f_conv: multiplier on exterior convection (reduced by R_value)
  • f_rad: multiplier on IR radiation to sky (cover blocks most LW)

Wall Insulation

Main.GreenhousesJL.WallInsulation — Type
WallInsulation(; U_wall, fraction, facade, name)

Replace part of the glass walls with insulated opaque construction.

Fields

  • U_wall — U-value of the insulated wall [W/(m²·K)]
  • fraction — fraction of wall area that is insulated (0–1)
  • facade — which wall(s): :north, :all, :north_east
  • name — description string

Presets

  • NoInsulation() — all glass walls (default)
  • InsulatedNorthWall() — north wall insulated (U=0.4), common in lean-to greenhouses
  • ChineseNorthWall() — massive earth/brick north wall (U=0.3, stores heat)
  • FullyInsulatedWalls() — all walls insulated (U=0.5), like a polytunnel with panels
  • PolycarbonateWalls() — double polycarbonate (U=2.5, still translucent)

Physics

Insulated walls have lower U-value but also block solar radiation. The net effect on heating depends on the balance between reduced losses and reduced solar gain.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for NoInsulation. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for InsulatedNorthWall. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for ChineseNorthWall. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for FullyInsulatedWalls. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for PolycarbonateWalls. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for has_insulation. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Main.GreenhousesJL.wall_U_effective — Function
wall_U_effective(insulation, U_glass, A_walls) → U_eff, A_insulated

Compute effective wall U-value accounting for mixed glass/insulated areas.

Thermal Screens

Main.GreenhousesJL.ThermalScreen — Type
ThermalScreen(; ε, porosity, deploy_below_irradiance, name)

Horizontal thermal screen deployed between the canopy and the glass cover. Standard in Dutch Venlo greenhouses.

Fields

  • ε — screen emissivity [-] (low ε = more reflective = better insulation)
  • porosity — air permeability (0 = sealed, 1 = fully open) [-]
  • deploy_below_irradiance — auto-deploy threshold [W/m²]

Presets

  • NoScreen() — no screen
  • StandardScreen() — typical aluminised screen (ε=0.3, saves ~40% heating)
  • BlackoutScreen() — opaque blackout (ε=0.9, for light control)
  • EnergyScreen() — high-performance aluminised (ε=0.15, saves ~60%)
Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for NoScreen. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for StandardScreen. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for EnergyScreen. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for BlackoutScreen. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for has_screen. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Main.GreenhousesJL.screen_factor — Function
screen_factor(screen, I_glob) → f_rad

Reduction factor on IR radiation from cover to sky when screen is deployed. The screen intercepts IR from the canopy/air before it reaches the cover.

Phase Change Materials

Main.GreenhousesJL.PCMStorage — Type
PCMStorage(; T_melt, L_fusion, m_per_m2, cp_solid, cp_liquid,
             λ_pcm, location, name)

Phase Change Material for thermal energy storage in the greenhouse.

PCMs absorb heat when melting (day/heating excess) and release it when solidifying (night), buffering temperature swings and reducing peak heating.

Fields

  • T_melt — melting temperature [°C]. Choose close to the setpoint.
  • L_fusion — latent heat of fusion [J/kg] (energy stored per kg during phase change)
  • m_per_m2 — PCM mass per m² of floor [kg/m²]
  • cp_solid — specific heat in solid phase [J/(kg·K)]
  • cp_liquid — specific heat in liquid phase [J/(kg·K)]
  • λ_pcm — thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)]
  • location — where the PCM is placed: :floor, :north_wall, :containers
  • name — description string

Physics

The PCM state is tracked by its enthalpy H_pcm [J/m²]:

  • Below melting: T_pcm = T_ref + H_pcm / (m · cp_solid)
  • During melting: T_pcm = T_melt, H increases from 0 to m · L_fusion
  • Above melting: T_pcm = T_melt + (H - m·L) / (m · cp_liquid)

Heat exchange with air: Q_pcm = h_pcm · (T_air - T_pcm) [W/m²] where h_pcm depends on the installation (convective surface).

Presets

  • NoPCM() — no PCM (default)
  • ParaffinFloor() — paraffin wax capsules under floor (T_melt=18°C)
  • SaltHydrateWall() — CaCl₂·6H₂O panels on north wall (T_melt=29°C)
  • BioPCMContainers() — bio-based PCM in containers (T_melt=21°C)
  • MicroencapsulatedCeiling() — microPCM integrated in ceiling panels (T_melt=23°C)

References

  • Berroug et al. (2011). Thermal performance of a greenhouse with a PCM north wall. Energy and Buildings, 43, 3027–3035.
  • Benli & Durmuş (2009). PCM integrated to greenhouse heating. Applied Energy, 86, 1913–1921.
  • Lazaar et al. (2014). PCM for heating a greenhouse. Energy Conversion and Management, 88, 1069–1076.
Missing docstring.

Missing docstring for NoPCM. Check Documenter's build log for details.

Main.GreenhousesJL.ParaffinFloor — Function

Paraffin wax capsules embedded under the floor slab (T_melt=18°C). Commercial: RT18HC (Rubitherm). L=250 kJ/kg, 15 kg/m².

Main.GreenhousesJL.SaltHydrateWall — Function

CaCl₂·6H₂O salt hydrate panels on north wall (T_melt=29°C). High latent heat (190 kJ/kg), cheap, but supercooling risk. 10 kg/m² of wall area ≈ 3 kg/m² floor for Savona geometry.

Main.GreenhousesJL.BioPCMContainers — Function

Bio-based PCM (coconut oil / fatty acids) in containers placed inside. T_melt=21°C, L=180 kJ/kg, 10 kg/m².

Main.GreenhousesJL.MicroencapsulatedCeiling — Function

Microencapsulated PCM in ceiling panels (Micronal by BASF). T_melt=23°C, L=110 kJ/kg, 5 kg/m². Good contact with warm air rising.

Main.GreenhousesJL.has_pcm — Function
has_pcm(p) → Bool

Whether phase change material storage is present.

Main.GreenhousesJL.pcm_temperature — Function
pcm_temperature(H_pcm, pcm) → T_pcm [°C]

Compute PCM temperature from its enthalpy state.

The enthalpy-temperature relationship has three regions:

  1. Solid: H < 0 → T < T_melt
  2. Melting: 0 ≤ H ≤ m·L → T = T_melt (isothermal)
  3. Liquid: H > m·L → T > T_melt
Main.GreenhousesJL.pcm_liquid_fraction — Function
pcm_liquid_fraction(H_pcm, pcm) → f ∈ [0, 1]

Fraction of PCM that has melted. 0 = fully solid, 1 = fully liquid.

Main.GreenhousesJL.pcm_heat_exchange — Function
pcm_heat_exchange(T_air_C, H_pcm, pcm) → (Q_to_pcm, T_pcm)

Heat flux from air to PCM [W/m²_floor] and current PCM temperature [°C].

Positive Q means air heats the PCM (charging). Negative means PCM releases heat (discharging).

Utilities

Main.GreenhousesJL.list_insulation_presets — Function
InsulationPresets

Summary of all available insulation presets.

Geometry Transfer

Intermediate format

Main.GreenhousesJL.Quad — Type
Quad(name, p1, p2, p3, p4, material_type)

A 3 or 4-vertex surface in Cartesian coordinates. p1..p4 are (x,y,z) tuples, counter-clockwise from outside. For triangles, set p4 = p3 (degenerate quad).

Main.GreenhousesJL.greenhouse_to_quads — Function
greenhouse_to_quads(p::GreenhouseParams) → Vector{Quad}

Extract all greenhouse surfaces as positioned quads in Cartesian coordinates. This is the universal intermediate representation — independent of the target tool (VPL, EnergyPlus, Blender, etc.).

Main.GreenhousesJL.plant_grid_positions — Function
plant_grid_positions(p::GreenhouseParams; n_plants=9) → Vector{NTuple{3,Float64}}

Compute plant positions (x, y, z=0) in a grid within the greenhouse footprint.

Main.GreenhousesJL.sensor_grid_positions — Function
sensor_grid_positions(p::GreenhouseParams; n_sensors=10) → Vector{NTuple{3,Float64}}

Compute sensor positions at floor level.

VPL transfer

Main.GreenhousesJL.greenhouse_to_vpl — Function
greenhouse_to_vpl(p::GreenhouseParams; n_plants=0, n_leaves=6) → (meshes, colors)

Full transfer: GreenhousesJL → VPL meshes with materials and colors. Returns merged mesh vertices and per-vertex colors for rendering.

PlantBiophysics transfer

Main.GreenhousesJL.greenhouse_to_plantbiophysics — Function
greenhouse_to_plantbiophysics(p, sol, t_index) → Dict

Extract greenhouse climate conditions at time step t_index from a simulation solution, formatted for PlantBiophysics.jl Atmosphere.

Returns a Dict with:

  • :T — air temperature [°C]
  • :Wind — indoor air speed [m/s] (estimated)
  • :P — atmospheric pressure [kPa]
  • :Rh — relative humidity [0-1]
  • :Cₛ — CO2 concentration [ppm]
  • :PPFD — PAR at canopy level [µmol/m²/s]
  • :Ra_SW_f — absorbed shortwave per leaf [W/m²]

These can be passed directly to PlantBiophysics Atmosphere() and ModelList().

Main.GreenhousesJL.greenhouse_timeseries_to_pb — Function
greenhouse_timeseries_to_pb(p, sol; every=1) → Vector{Dict}

Extract a time series of PlantBiophysics-compatible conditions from a greenhouse simulation.

every: sample every N time steps (default: every step).

EPW Weather Files

Main.GreenhousesJL.EPWData — Type
EPWData

Extended weather data from an EPW file, including direct/diffuse split.

Main.GreenhousesJL.load_epw — Function
load_epw(filepath) → EPWData

Load an EnergyPlus Weather file (.epw) with full radiation data.

Main.GreenhousesJL.epw_to_tmy — Function
epw_to_tmy(epw::EPWData; start_month=1, n_days=365) → TMYData

Convert EPW data to GreenhousesJL TMYData format. Optionally select a date range by start_month and n_days.

Main.GreenhousesJL.epw_summary — Function
epw_summary(epw::EPWData)

Print a summary of the EPW weather data.

Soil Library

Main.GreenhousesJL.SoilType — Type
SoilType(; name, λ_dry, λ_sat, ρ_dry, cp_dry, porosity, moisture)

Soil thermal properties for a specific soil texture.

Fields

  • name — soil type name
  • λ_dry — dry thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)]
  • λ_sat — saturated thermal conductivity [W/(m·K)]
  • ρ_dry — dry bulk density [kg/m³]
  • cp_dry — dry specific heat [J/(kg·K)]
  • porosity — porosity [m³/m³]
  • moisture — default volumetric water content [m³/m³]

Effective properties are computed as function of moisture content using the de Vries (1963) interpolation.

Main.GreenhousesJL.Sand — Function

Sandy soil — high conductivity, low water retention.

Main.GreenhousesJL.Loam — Function

Loam — balanced texture, typical greenhouse soil.

Main.GreenhousesJL.Clay — Function

Clay — low conductivity, high capacity.

Main.GreenhousesJL.Peat — Function

Peat / organic — very low conductivity, high porosity.

Main.GreenhousesJL.Gravel — Function

Gravel — very high conductivity, low capacity.

Main.GreenhousesJL.ConcreteSlab — Function

Concrete slab (greenhouse floor) — high conductivity.

Main.GreenhousesJL.effective_conductivity — Function
effective_conductivity(soil::SoilType; moisture=nothing) → λ [W/(m·K)]

Compute effective thermal conductivity using Johansen (1975) / de Vries method.

λ_eff = λ_dry + (λ_sat - λ_dry) × Ke

where Ke is the Kersten number (function of saturation degree).

Main.GreenhousesJL.effective_capacity — Function
effective_capacity(soil::SoilType; moisture=nothing) → ρcp [J/(m³·K)]

Compute effective volumetric heat capacity.

ρcp = ρ_dry × cp_dry + ρ_water × cp_water × θ

Water adds 4.18 MJ/(m³·K) per unit moisture.

Main.GreenhousesJL.soil_to_params — Function
soil_to_params(soil::SoilType, concrete::SoilType=ConcreteSlab();
               A, N_c=2, N_s=5, T_deep) → SoilParams

Convert soil types to GreenhousesJL SoilParams.

Main.GreenhousesJL.kusuda_temperature — Function
kusuda_temperature(depth, day_of_year; T_mean, T_amp, t_shift, α_soil) → T [°C]

Kusuda & Achenbach (1965) undisturbed ground temperature at depth z.

\[T(z, t) = T_{mean} - T_{amp} \cdot e^{-z \sqrt{\pi / (365 \cdot \alpha)}} \cdot \cos\left(\frac{2\pi}{365}(t - t_{shift} - \frac{z}{2}\sqrt{\frac{365}{\pi \alpha}})\right)\]

Arguments

  • depth — depth below surface [m]
  • day_of_year — day number (1–365)
  • T_mean — annual mean surface temperature [°C]
  • T_amp — annual surface temperature amplitude [°C] (half of max-min)
  • t_shift — day of minimum surface temperature (typically ~35 for Northern Hemisphere)
  • α_soil — soil thermal diffusivity [m²/s] (default 0.5e-6 for typical soil)
Main.GreenhousesJL.kusuda_deep_temperature — Function
kusuda_deep_temperature(T_annual_mean, T_annual_amp; depth=2.0, day_of_year=1) → T_deep [K]

Estimate the deep soil boundary temperature from annual mean and amplitude using the Kusuda model at the specified depth.

At 2 m depth, the annual oscillation is damped by ~80%.

Main.GreenhousesJL.list_soil_types — Function
list_soil_types()

Print all available soil type presets with their properties.

ODE System

Core

Main.GreenhousesJL.greenhouse_rhs! — Function
greenhouse_rhs!(du, u, p::GreenhouseParams, t)

In-place ODE right-hand side for the greenhouse thermal model. Compatible with any SciML solver (ODEProblem, solve).

State vector layout (indices vary with configuration):

  1. T_cov [K], 2. T_air [K], 3. T_flr [K],
  2. T_wall [K] (if walls), 5. T_canopy [K] (if crop),

6–8. C_Buf, C_Leaf, C_Stem [mg/m²] (if growth), then soil layers, PI integral, PV cumulative energy.

Main.GreenhousesJL.initial_state — Function
initial_state(p::GreenhouseParams; T0=278.15, T_soil0=276.15) → Vector{Float64}

Generate a consistent initial state vector.

Arguments

  • T0 — initial temperature [K] for cover, air, floor, walls, canopy (default: 5°C)
  • T_soil0 — initial temperature [K] for all soil layers (default: 3°C)

Heat Transfer

Main.GreenhousesJL.h_conv_ext — Function
h_conv_ext(u, ϕ; ΔT=0.0) → W/(m²·K)

External convection coefficient on a greenhouse cover.

Combines a forced-convection part (Bot 1983 / OutsideAirConvection.mo — wind-driven, validated against greenhouse measurements) with a natural-convection part (ASHRAE for buoyancy-driven flow on a horizontal-up surface) using the DOE-2 quadratic combination:

h_total = √(h_forced² + h_natural²)

The natural part is essential at low wind: without it the cover loses 30-50 % less heat to the outside than EnergyPlus / DOE-2 expect, which leaves the greenhouse a few degrees too hot during the day in sheltered climates (e.g. alpine valleys with summer wind ≤ 2 m/s).

Pass ΔT = T_cov - T_out to enable the natural term; the default ΔT = 0 preserves the original Bot-only behavior for backwards compatibility.

Main.GreenhousesJL.h_conv_free — Function

Interior free convection coefficient [W/(m².K)] — Nu-Ra for inclined surfaces.

Matches FreeConvection.mo (Balemans 1989):

  • Inclined surface (cover): HEC = 1.7 × |ΔT|^0.33 × cos(ϕ)^(-0.66)
  • Floor upward (Tflr > Tair): HEC = 1.7 × |ΔT|^0.33
  • Floor downward (Tflr < Tair): HEC = 1.3 × |ΔT|^0.25

Smooth sigmoid transition (s=11) between upward/downward on floor.

Main.GreenhousesJL.q_rad_T4 — Function

Long-wave radiation heat flux [W/m²] from surface a to b.

Output

Main.GreenhousesJL.export_csv — Function
export_csv(filepath, sol, p)

Export simulation results to a CSV file with human-readable columns.

Columns: timeh, TairC, TcoverC, TfloorC, ToutC, TskyC, IglobWm2, uwindms, QheatWm2, EheatkWh (+ Twall_C if walls are present)

Main.GreenhousesJL.plot_results — Function
plot_results(sol, p::GreenhouseParams; save_path=nothing)

Plot time series (temperatures, weather, heating). Requires using GLMakie or using CairoMakie before calling.

Main.GreenhousesJL.view_greenhouse_3d — Function
view_greenhouse_3d(p::GreenhouseParams; save_path=nothing)

Interactive 3D view of the greenhouse geometry. Requires using GLMakie or using CairoMakie before calling.
